extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. The only transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (send_attempt under guard "not opted_out"), which can fire only when opted_out is false and leaves opted_out unchanged at false; thus immediately after t2 the antecedent is false and the property is satisfied. The only way to make opted_out true is t1 (optout), which simultaneously sets sent:=false. opted_out is monotonic — no transition ever resets it to false — so once true it stays true. In every state with opted_out=true: t2 is disabled (its guard requires not opted_out), while send_attempt instead matches t3 (sent:=false), tick matches t4 (sent:=false), and optout matches t1 (sent:=false). Therefore no enabled transition can set sent=true while opted_out is true, and the state (opted_out=true, sent=true) is unreachable. The ticks variable is irrelevant to the property. Verified for executions up to 5 events (and in fact for all lengths, since the argument is an inductive invariant). No counterexample exists.
